architecture_fit.note: Hand-built exp(-d/lambda) causal head directly produces distance-local attention and fits the goal's synthetic pure-NumPy model_fn contract.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on the goal's analytic uniform baseline, which it clearly beats (slope 1.84 vs 0.24, lift 1.60); no extra strawman added.

faithfulness.note: Weights are hand-constructed, not used by any model; README never acknowledges this or proposes a causal/ablation check as the rubric asks for synthetic attempts.

operating_range.note: Decay holds smoothly across bins 0-32 (>1 order of magnitude), but no explicit range sweep or breakdown of where it degrades.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is a hand-set circuit reproducing the target distance-decay behaviour — exactly the bonus criterion, though trivial and identical across all layers/heads.

visual_judgement.note: App fails to boot: benchmark_panel(load_task(...)) passes a module not a path; Demo is only a grayscale attention-matrix image, not a decay-vs-baseline comparison.

visualisation_rationale.note: The 'Why this visualisation' section is generic and does not justify axes or tie the chart to the goal's baseline-comparison grain.

notes: The hand-built exp(-d/lambda) causal head genuinely solves the goal's synthetic measurement, decaying fast with distance and strongly beating the uniform baseline (headline 1.84 vs 0.24, 97% local mass). However it is a trivial duplicated pattern with no per-layer/head differentiation, no causal/faithfulness grounding or even acknowledgement of its synthetic nature, and the Gradio app fails to boot because benchmark_panel is handed a module instead of the goal directory. Solid metrics, weak faithfulness and a broken visualisation hold it to borderline.
